title: XML External Entity (XXE)
principle: Turn off DTDs and external entity resolution in every XML parser that touches untrusted input. External entities let an attacker read local files, reach internal services (SSRF), or exhaust resources. Most parsers are unsafe by default; disable doctypes explicitly, or use a hardened wrapper.

snippets.vulnerable:
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
tree = ET.fromstring(untrusted)
snippets.secure:
from defusedxml.ElementTree import fromstring
tree = fromstring(untrusted)
snippets.note: defusedxml disables entities and billion-laughs expansion. For lxml, parse with etree.XMLParser(resolve_entities=False, no_network=True).

snippets.vulnerable:
DocumentBuilderFactory f = DocumentBuilderFactory.newInstance();
Document doc = f.newDocumentBuilder().parse(input);
snippets.note: disallow-doctype-decl is the strongest single switch. Apply the same features to SAXParserFactory, XMLInputFactory (StAX: IS_SUPPORTING_EXTERNAL_ENTITIES=false), and Transformer.

snippets.library: libxml with network access and entity substitution off
snippets.secure:
$doc = new DOMDocument();
$doc->loadXML($untrusted, LIBXML_NONET);   // no network; do not pass LIBXML_NOENT
snippets.note: LIBXML_NONET blocks external fetches; never enable LIBXML_NOENT (it substitutes entities). On PHP < 8.0 also call libxml_disable_entity_loader(true).

snippets.vulnerable: // A custom parser that follows <!ENTITY ... SYSTEM "..."> references
snippets.secure:
var v Doc
err := xml.Unmarshal(untrusted, &v)   // encoding/xml ignores external entities by default
snippets.note: Go's encoding/xml does not expand external or remote entities, so it is safe by default. Only a custom decoder that sets Decoder.Entity or fetches SYSTEM ids reintroduces XXE.

references.title: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
